adit: noun. A horizontal or nearly horizontal passage from the surface into a mine, as contrasted with a shaft or vertical entry passage, used
aid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Alngith.
air: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Airoran.
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
art: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for artificial languages.
dah: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gwahatike.
dart: noun. A pointed missile weapon, intended to be thrown by the hand; for example, a short lance or javelin.
daw: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Davawenyo.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
draw: verb. Senses relating to exerting force or pulling.
had: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hatam.
hair: noun. A pigmented filament of keratin which grows from a follicle on the skin of humans and other mammals.
hart: noun. A male deer, especially the male of the red deer after his fifth year.
hat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Haitian Creole.
haw: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hawaiian.
hid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hidatsa.
hit: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hittite.
rad: symbol. radian, unit of measure for angle
rah: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rabha.
raid: noun. A quick hostile or predatory incursion or invasion in a battle.
rat: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Razajerdi.
rath: noun. A walled enclosure, especially in Ireland; a ringfort built sometime between the Iron Age and the Viking Age.
raw: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rawang.
ria: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reang.
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
tad: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tause.
tahr: noun. Any of three genera of large Asian ungulates belonging to the subfamily Caprinae (goat-antelopes).
tar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Tarahumara.
taw: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tai.
thaw: verb. To gradually cause (something frozen, such as earth, ice, or snow) to melt or soften by raising the temperature.
third: adjective. The ordinal form of the cardinal number three; Coming after the second.
twa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Twana.
wad: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Wandamen.
wadi: noun. A valley, gully, or stream bed in northern Africa and southwest Asia that remains dry except during the rainy season.
wair: noun. A plank six feet long and one foot across.
wait: verb. To delay movement or action until some event or time; to remain neglected or in readiness.
war: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Waray-Waray.
ward: noun. A warden; a guard; a guardian or watchman.
wart: noun. A type of deformed growth occurring on the skin caused by the human papillomavirus (HPV).
wat: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kaninuwa.
waw: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Waiwai.
wha: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Manusela.
what: determiner. Which, especially which of an open-ended set of possibilities.
whid: noun. A quick motion; a rapid, quiet movement, usually by small game.
whit: noun. The smallest part or particle imaginable; an iota.
width: noun. The state of being wide.
wit: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Wintu.
withdraw: verb. To draw or pull (something) away or back from its original position or situation.
wraith: noun. A ghost or specter, especially a person's likeness seen just after their death.
wrath: noun. Great anger; an instance of this.
writ: noun. A written order, issued by a court, ordering someone to do (or stop doing) something.
